title: Multiple Students — Loop Processing
description: Wrap the student entry in a loop so the user can enter multiple students. After each student, ask 'Add another student? (y/n)' and continue based on the response. Also keep a running count of how many students were entered.
test_code:
cpp_lower = cpp.lower()
assert 'while' in cpp_lower or 'for' in cpp.split('main')[1], 'Must use a while or for loop to process multiple students'
assert 'cin' in cpp, 'Must use cin to read user input'
assert 'y' in cpp_lower and 'n' in cpp_lower, 'Must check for y/n response to continue or stop'
assert 'getline' in cpp, 'Must use getline to read student names'
assert 'if' in cpp and 'else' in cpp, 'Must still include if/else for letter grade logic'
assert 'count' in cpp_lower or '++' in cpp, 'Must track the number of students entered'
acceptance_criteria: Uses a while or do-while loop for repeated entry
Reads student name and grades inside the loop
Calculates average and letter grade for each student
Asks 'Add another?' and checks for y/n
Displays total student count at the end
hints: A do-while loop runs at least once: do { ... } while (choice == 'y');
After cin >>, use cin.ignore() before the next getline
Ask: cout << "Add another student? (y/n): "; cin >> choice;
